title: leontodon croceus subsp. rilaensis
intro: Leontodon croceus subsp. rilaensis, also known as the Balkan hawk's beard, is a rare and endangered subspecies of the Hawkbit plant, belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The health benefits of Leontodon croceus subsp. rilaensis include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant can be dried and used as a tea, or its roots and leaves can be used in infusions, capsules, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
